summary: End-to-end testing of GPSC Gujarat PYQ MVP. Backend: 23/23 pytest cases passed (auth, questions CRUD with admin gating, attempts scoring, stats, bookmarks, AI explain + AI generate via Gemini 3 Flash). Frontend: All critical UI flows passed - Landing, Browse with Gujarati search + filters, Login (admin), Admin panel listing, Practice with reveal + Next/Prev, Bookmark toggle, Bookmarks page, Mock setup, Dashboard, Signup auto-login, and AI Explanation rendering in Gujarati.

minor.issue: Occasional transient 500 'Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0.0, Max budget: 0.0' from upstream LLM gateway. Retried and passed. Likely upstream rate/budget hiccup, not app code.

critical_code_review_comments: MCQCard ai-explain endpoint is unauthenticated (POST /api/ai/explain has no Depends(require_user)). This exposes the LLM call to anonymous users and could lead to abuse/cost. Consider requiring auth OR adding rate limiting.
Backend /api/ai/explain returns the raw LLM exception substring (str(e)[:200]) in the 500 detail. Acceptable for now but consider redacting in production.
Frontend stores JWT in localStorage (gpsc_token); fine for this MVP but susceptible to XSS. Consider httpOnly cookies later.
server.py is 738 lines and houses models + routes + seeding. Splitting into modules (auth.py, questions.py, ai.py, models.py) would improve maintainability.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@gpscpyq.in / Admin@123 (seeded automatically). Regular users created on the fly with TEST_ prefix.
seed_data_creation: Backend auto-seeds 12 questions across 3 exams/3 years/6 subjects and 1 admin user on startup if collections are empty. Verified present and queryable.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is reusable - uses pytest with shared fixtures (admin_token, user_token). All Gujarati search, AI explain, AI generate, attempt scoring, bookmarks and admin gating are covered. AI explain occasionally hits a transient 'Budget exceeded' from the LLM gateway - retry usually clears it. Frontend uses kebab-case data-testid throughout (login-submit, signup-submit, start-practice-btn, option-{qid}-{idx}, ai-explain-btn-{qid}, ai-explanation-{qid}, bookmark-btn-{qid}, admin-save-btn, admin-delete-{qid}). Signup page does NOT expose data-testid on inputs - tests fell back to input[type='email'] / [type='text'] / [type='password'].
